summary: First-iteration full E2E test of LumiKids Academy Management System. 28/28 backend pytest cases pass (100%). All listed frontend flows render and integrate successfully: landing page, enrollment modal+submit, login for admin/teacher/parent, admin 6 sub-pages with user/class/student/enrollment CRUD via UI, teacher 5 sub-pages with attendance recording UI, parent dashboard with 4 sub-pages (Dashboard/My Child/Attendance/Messages), and Emma↔David messaging round-trip. Zero console errors observed. Role-based access correctly returns 403 for parent/teacher hitting /api/admin/* endpoints.

minor.issue: allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False — works for current Bearer-token flow, but brittle if cookie-based auth is added later.
No protection against an admin deleting their own account; could lock out the only admin. Consider guard.
No way to change a user's role via update payload (UserUpdateIn omits role).

Allows fetching a thread between any two users (e.g., parent fetching another parent's id) since no contact-restriction check; not a security risk for content (only own pair returned), but receiver_id is also unvalidated against contacts list on send.
If existing user has a non-parent role (e.g., teacher with same email), code still resets their password but does not change role; they keep teacher role with new student linked.

issues: Soft pastel theme renders cleanly with adequate contrast; sidebar active state and primary buttons readable.

action_items: (Optional) Allow admin role updates via PUT /api/admin/users; add self-delete guard.
(Optional) Validate receiver_id in POST /api/messages against /messages/contacts to prevent arbitrary user messaging.
(Optional) On enrollment approval where existing user has non-parent role, decide on policy (skip / convert / error).
critical_code_review_comments: AdminDashboard.jsx is large; consider splitting into per-resource files (Users/Students/Classes/Enrollments) for maintainability.
server.py is ~780 lines and exceeds the 700-line guideline; split into routers/users.py, routers/admin.py, routers/teacher.py, routers/parent.py, routers/messages.py.
/api/messages POST does not enforce that the conversation pair is a permitted contact (low-impact but worth tightening).
JWT_SECRET hardcoded default is committed in /app/backend/.env; that's expected for the dev preview but should be rotated for production.
SMTP credentials are stored in plaintext in /app/backend/.env (acknowledged - real Gmail). Should be moved to a secret manager in production.
test_credentials: /app/memory/test_credentials.md - admin@lumikids.com/admin123, sarah@lumikids.com/teacher123, david@lumikids.com/teacher123, emma@example.com/parent123, michael@example.com/parent123. All valid and produce JWTs with correct role claims.
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_-prefixed records during testing (users, classes, students, enrollment requests, messages). They are namespaced and can be left or cleaned up. One enrollment was approved end-to-end producing a temp_password successfully.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite at /app/backend/tests/test_lumikids.py covers auth/roles/CRUD/teacher/parent/messaging/notifications. Run via `pytest backend/tests/test_lumikids.py -v`. Frontend uses data-testid liberally — all key flows are accessible by testid (login-email, login-password, login-submit, enroll-* family, add-user-btn, user-form, user-save-btn, attendance-class-select, attendance-${id}-${status}, parent-message-input, etc.). Note: Parent dashboard has only 4 sub-pages (Dashboard/My Child/Attendance/Messages) — there is no separate 'Reports' tab; reports info is shown inside the attendance/child views. SMTP runs as a fire-and-forget BackgroundTask so it never blocks API responses; test suite ignores actual delivery.
rca_of_the_issue: No critical issues. The single 'failure' surfaced during UI exploration (Parent → Reports tab not found) was a misconception of the spec — the parent role only exposes 4 sub-pages and 'Reports' is not one of them; this matches the requested coverage.
